notes:
dí = to chase closely or press something right up against someone/something. It feels more intense and close than just “follow.”

As a verb, it often suggests very little distance — either physically chasing or pushing something tight against a surface.  

Compared to đuổi (chase), dí feels closer and more aggressive — almost “right on someone’s heels.” It can also imply pressure, not just movement.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The baby laughs when his brother pretends to chase him around the room.
The cat likes to chase the laser light on the floor.
The little kid is afraid of being chased so he runs quickly out of the yard.
exampleSentences: Em bé cười khi anh trai giả vờ dí em quanh phòng.
Con mèo thích dí theo ánh đèn laser trên sàn nhà.
Bạn nhỏ sợ bị dí nên chạy rất nhanh ra khỏi sân.
notes_md:
**dí** = to chase closely or press something right up against someone/something. It feels more intense and close than just “follow.”

As a verb, it often suggests very little distance — either physically chasing or pushing something tight against a surface.  

Compared to **đuổi** (chase), *dí* feels closer and more aggressive — almost “right on someone’s heels.” It can also imply pressure, not just movement.

definitions.vi: Động từ chỉ hành động đuổi theo sát nút hoặc ấn vật gì đó sát vào bề mặt/người khác với áp lực mạnh.
definitions.en: To chase closely; to press against someone or something.

content.sentence: Con mèo thích ___ theo ánh đèn laser trên sàn nhà.

translation.en: The cat likes to chase the laser light on the floor.

content.sentence: Em bé cười khi anh trai giả vờ ___ em quanh phòng.

translation.en: The baby laughs when his brother pretends to chase him around the room.
